lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Lore v1 Rewrite ]─ ┐

Lore v1 Rewrite Plan

Clean rewrite of the Lore framework from 86 files / 11,000 lines down to ~15 files / ~800 lines. Focus on the core self-learning loop. No multi-tier, no MkDocs, no roadmap infrastructure.

Design reference: Lore v1 Rewrite Brainstorm (archived in lorehq-legacy)

Task List

Phase 0: Repo Setup

  • 0.1 Complete local renames, remote updates, and clone new repos
    • See next-steps.md for exact commands
    • What’s done: GitHub renames (lore→lore-legacy, lore-lorehq→lorehq-legacy), new repos created (lore, create-lore), local rename lore-lorehq→lorehq-legacy
    • What’s left: rename local lore→lore-legacy, update both legacy remotes, clone new lore and create-lore
  • 0.2 Commit this brainstorm and plan to lorehq-legacy and push
  • 0.3 Verify all 4 repos exist on GitHub: lore, create-lore, lore-legacy, lorehq-legacy
  • 0.4 Verify local directories: lore/, create-lore/, lore-legacy/, lorehq-legacy/

Phase 1: Installer (create-lore repo)

  • 1.1 Create package.json with name: "create-lore", bin entry, MIT license
  • 1.2 Create bin/create-lore.js — minimal CLI that clones lore repo to temp, copies to target dir, cleans up
    • Parse args: name (required), target dir (optional, defaults to ./lore-<name>)
    • Interactive prompt: “Which AI coding tools?” (multi-select: Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode)
    • Store selection in .lore-config
    • See brainstorm §6.3
  • 1.3 Create test/create-lore.test.js — acceptance test
    • Runs installer against current lore repo
    • Asserts expected files exist
    • Asserts hooks are wired in .claude/settings.json
    • Asserts AGENTS.md is present and non-empty
    • Runs scripts/validate-consistency.sh in output
    • Cleans up output directory
    • See brainstorm §8
  • 1.4 Verify npx create-lore testproject works end-to-end locally

Phase 2: Core Instructions (lore repo)

  • 2.1 Write AGENTS.md (~80 lines) — stripped-down operating instructions
    • Repository purpose (meta-repo for agent knowledge, not app code)
    • Operating principles (3-point: create as needed, build knowledge, document everything)
    • Skill creation rule (“every gotcha becomes a skill, no exceptions”)
    • Skill split rules (one interaction method, ~80 line limit)
    • Agent creation rule (domain = agent, 1:1)
    • Delegation rule (delegate by domain, check agent-registry)
    • Knowledge management (docs-first, memory routing)
    • Reconciliation checklist (capture knowledge, create skills, validate)
    • NO: multi-tier hierarchy, MkDocs, roadmaps/plans, documentation tiers, knowledge map, model preferences, hook descriptions, orchestrator skill list
    • See brainstorm §4
  • 2.2 Create CLAUDE.md — initially identical to AGENTS.md (generate script comes later)
  • 2.3 Create .lore-config — just name and created fields, nothing else
  • 2.4 Run installer test — should pass file existence checks

Phase 3: Hooks (lore repo)

  • 3.1 Create .claude/settings.json — wire all 4 hook events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure)
  • 3.2 Create hooks/session-init.js (~40 lines)
    • Load agent domains from agent-registry.md
    • Load MEMORY.local.md if present
    • Output operating principles summary
    • NO: roadmap scanning, multi-tier work paths, level detection
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/hooks/session-init.js (166 lines) for logic, strip to essentials
  • 3.3 Create hooks/prompt-preamble.js (~12 lines)
    • Terse delegation + task list reminder
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/hooks/prompt-preamble.js (can reuse as-is)
  • 3.4 Create hooks/protect-memory.js (~48 lines)
    • Intercept Write/Edit targeting MEMORY.md
    • Force routing decision: skill, docs/environment, or MEMORY.local.md
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/hooks/protect-memory.js (can reuse as-is)
  • 3.5 Create hooks/post-edit-reminder.js (~60 lines)
    • Fire after every tool use with contextual nudge
    • Silent for read-only tools (Read, Grep, Glob)
    • Track consecutive bash calls, escalate at 3+ and 5+
    • Silent when writing to knowledge paths (docs/, .claude/skills/)
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/hooks/post-edit-reminder.js (139 lines) — simplify
  • 3.6 Run installer test — should pass hook wiring checks

Phase 4: Skills (lore repo)

  • 4.1 Create .claude/skills/create-skill/SKILL.md (~100 lines)
    • Simplified skill creation process
    • NO: level prefixes, contribution workflow, domain hierarchy
    • Must call scripts/generate-registries.sh after creation
    • Must trigger agent creation check (does domain have an agent?)
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/.claude/skills/create-skill/SKILL.md (184 lines) — strip multi-tier
  • 4.2 Create .claude/skills/create-agent/SKILL.md (~80 lines)
    • Simplified agent creation process
    • NO: level prefixes, model field templating
    • Must call scripts/generate-registries.sh after creation
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/.claude/skills/create-agent/SKILL.md (137 lines) — strip multi-tier

Phase 5: Scripts (lore repo)

  • 5.1 Create scripts/generate-registries.sh (~80 lines)
    • Scan .claude/skills/ and .claude/agents/
    • Generate agent-registry.md and skills-registry.md
    • NO: level-aware grouping, prefix handling
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/scripts/generate-registries.sh (335 lines) — strip to core
  • 5.2 Create scripts/generate-agents.sh (~60 lines)
    • Group skills by domain, generate agent markdown files
    • NO: level-aware naming, model overrides
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/scripts/generate-agents.sh (199 lines) — strip to core
  • 5.3 Create scripts/validate-consistency.sh (~100 lines)
    • Check: every skill in registry, every agent in registry, agent domains match skills
    • Check: no orphan agents without skills, no orphan registry entries
    • NO: MkDocs nav checks, doc claims validation, work hierarchy linting
    • Reference: lorehq-legacy/scripts/validate-consistency.sh (511 lines) — strip to core
  • 5.4 Create empty agent-registry.md and skills-registry.md (generated on first skill creation)
  • 5.5 Run installer test — should pass full validation

Phase 6: End-to-End Verification

  • 6.1 Run npx create-lore testproject from scratch
  • 6.2 Open Claude Code in the generated project, verify:
    • Session init fires and shows operating principles
    • Post-edit reminder fires after tool use
    • Memory guard intercepts MEMORY.md writes
    • /create-skill works and updates registries
    • /create-agent works and generates agent file
    • scripts/validate-consistency.sh passes
  • 6.3 Publish create-lore to npm (initial 0.1.0)
  • 6.4 Test npx create-lore from npm (not local)

Phase 7: Reconciliation Checkpoint

  • 7.1 Review: Is anything in the new framework that shouldn’t be?
  • 7.2 Review: Is anything missing that blocks the self-learning loop?
  • 7.3 Update create-lore README with usage instructions
  • 7.4 Tag lore repo with v0.1.0

Phase 8: Work Management & Docs

  • 8.1 Create create-roadmap skill (~30 lines) — operator-initiated roadmap creation
  • 8.2 Create create-plan skill (~35 lines) — standalone or nested under roadmap
  • 8.3 Create create-brainstorm skill (~25 lines) — always standalone, no status field
  • 8.4 Create capture skill (~40 lines) — replaces legacy reconcile, user-invocable as /capture
  • 8.5 Create consolidate skill (~60 lines) — replaces legacy housekeep, user-invocable as /consolidate
  • 8.6 Create create-todo-with-capture skill (~15 lines) — replaces legacy create-todo-with-reconciliation
  • 8.7 Modify hooks/session-init.js — scan and display active roadmaps/plans in session banner
  • 8.8 Modify scripts/generate-nav.sh — add work/ section, exclude archive/ dirs
  • 8.9 Update CLAUDE.md — add Work Management section, update File Layout
  • 8.10 Add README.md — 3 core principles (Self-Learning, Delegation, Work Continuity), quickstart
  • 8.11 Update docs/index.md — docs site homepage matching README
  • 8.12 Sweep v1 repo for legacy multi-tier contamination — clean (only scope: internal found, kept intentionally)
  • 8.13 All verification checks pass (registries, validation, nav, installer tests 6/6, session-init graceful with no work/ dir)

Future Phases (not in this plan)

These are documented in the brainstorm §7 and will become separate plans when ready:

  • Environmental knowledge capture: docs/environment/, routing expansion
  • Multi-tool support: Cursor + OpenCode adapters
  • Docs UI: MkDocs

Reference Material

  • Design decisions: Lore v1 Rewrite Brainstorm
  • Old framework source: lorehq/lore-legacy on GitHub
  • Old operational instance: lorehq/lorehq-legacy on GitHub
  • OpenCode plugins (to port later): ~/Github/chappie-homelab/.opencode/plugins/
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
